data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Apple's site is likely using a microservices architecture allowing different sections like Store, Support, and Account to operate independently.
data.technical_spec: A decoupled service architecture utilizing container systems like Kubernetes for scalability.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Apple employs a global CDN to deliver content quickly across the world.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of services such as Akamai or Apple's own CDN to cache and distribute content.

data.title: Responsive Design
data.description: The website is designed to be fully responsive, ensuring a seamless experience across all device types.
data.technical_spec: Frameworks like Bootstrap or custom CSS media queries.

data.title: High Availability
data.description: Ensures constant uptime and performance even during high-traffic events like product launches.
data.technical_spec: Load balancing technologies and failover strategies including multi-region deployments.

data.title: Progressive Web App (PWA) Elements
data.description: Implementation of PWA technologies for certain site features, enhancing mobile and offline capabilities.
data.technical_spec: Use of Service Workers and Web App Manifests.
